title: alnus alnobetula var. genuina
intro: Alnus alnobetula var. genuina, also known as green alder, is a subspecies of alder tree native to boreal forests in North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. It has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, as well as having antimicrobial properties that can aid in wound healing. The herbal preparations of Alnus alnobetula var. genuina include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
